name: Fashion
description: Designers, brands, icons, and style history

questions.q: Who is credited with popularizing the 'little black dress'?

source.notes: Popularized in 1926 via American Vogue. Called it the 'Ford' of dresses — universally flattering.

questions.q: What was Coco Chanel's actual first name?

source.notes: Full name Gabrielle Bonheur Chanel. 'Coco' was a nickname from her early years as a cabaret singer.

questions.q: Which luxury designer is famous for shoes with distinctive red soles?

source.notes: Started painting soles red in 1993. Successfully trademarked the color in the EU and US.

questions.q: Which shoe brand was obsessively referenced by Carrie Bradshaw in 'Sex and the City'?

source.notes: Spanish designer. Carrie was robbed at gunpoint for her blue Manolos in Season 3.

questions.q: Which designer popularized the 'New Look' silhouette after World War II?

source.notes: Unveiled 1947. Featured full skirts, nipped waists — a reaction to wartime austerity.

questions.q: Who succeeded Gianni Versace as Creative Director of Versace after his death in 1997?

source.notes: His younger sister. She had been with the brand since the 1970s.

questions.q: Which iconic 1960s model was known as 'Twiggy'?

source.notes: Born Lesley Hornby. Became face of Swinging London at 16. Nickname from her thin figure.

questions.q: Which former Spice Girl started her own namesake fashion label in 2008?

source.notes: Posh Spice. Label became critically acclaimed in fashion circles.

questions.q: DKNY is a more accessible diffusion line of which designer?

source.notes: DKNY stands for 'Donna Karan New York.' Launched 1989.

questions.q: Vera Wang is primarily known for designing what type of garment?

source.notes: Former figure skater and Vogue editor. Launched her bridal line in 1990.

questions.q: Which designer's iconic logo features two C's facing each other?

source.notes: Introduced 1925. The 'Double C' logo (sometimes called the 'Bonheur Cross').

questions.q: In what year did the first modern bra as we know it get patented?

source.notes: Patent 1914 by Mary Phelps Jacob (Caresse Crosby). Cup sizing (A-D) standardized 1935.

questions.q: Which American First Lady in the 1960s became a major global fashion icon?

source.notes: Pillbox hats and Oleg Cassini suits. Her style influence was called 'the Jackie look.'

questions.q: Which fashion designer launched Yves Saint Laurent as a brand in 1961 after his departure from Dior?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: softened 'leaving Dior' to 'departure from Dior' (he was dismissed in 1960 while serving in the French army). Took over as Dior's chief designer at 21 after Christian Dior's death in 1957.

questions.q: Which fashion house is known for its iconic double-G logo and created the Bamboo handbag in 1947?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: fixed garbled 'Breaking the Bamboo' wording and corrected original_a (was 'Ralph Lauren'). Double G logo. Founded 1921 in Florence by Guccio Gucci; Bamboo Bag debuted 1947.

questions.q: Which designer brand created the famous 'Polo' pony logo?

source.notes: Launched 1967. Lauren's original name was Ralph Lifshitz.

questions.q: What iconic green crocodile is the logo for which clothing brand?

source.notes: Founded by tennis player René Lacoste in 1933. He was nicknamed 'The Crocodile.'

questions.q: Which Japanese designer revolutionized fashion by launching his brand in Paris in 1970?

source.notes: First Japanese designer to show in Paris. Founded the Kenzo label in 1970.

questions.q: What is the traditional season for haute couture fashion shows in Paris?

source.notes: Haute couture shows happen twice a year. Regular ready-to-wear shows are in February and September.

questions.q: What is the famous annual Met Gala held at the Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York a fundraiser for?

source.notes: Anna Wintour has organized it since 1995. Each year has a specific theme.
